overview: This is a Madagascar giraffe weevil (Trachelophorus giraffa), an insect native to the forests of Madagascar. The male has an extremely elongated neck, which it uses for fighting rivals and building nests. They are herbivores that feed on leaves and are known for rolling them into nests to protect their eggs. Despite their strange appearance, they are harmless to humans, unable to bite or sting. This is a Madagascar giraffe weevil (Trachelophorus giraffa), an insect native to the forests of Madagascar.
The male has an extremely elongated neck, which it uses for fighting rivals and building nests.
They are herbivores that feed on leaves and are known for rolling them into nests to protect their eggs.
Despite their strange appearance, they are harmless to humans, unable to bite or sting.
